title: xeraea regeliana
intro: Xeraea regeliana, also known as Yerba de Mayo, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves have been used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In traditional medicine, Yerba de Mayo is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving stress, and boosting the immune system.
